_comment: The universal Interaction catalog (Article XXXVI.2). Every entity in the game exposes interactions from this shared catalog. This is the common language the AI uses to engage the world, and the verbs the player sees in the conversation UI. Not every interaction applies to every entity â€” an NPC's data specifies which interactions they expose, gated by relationship/knowledge/cultivation (Article XXXVII). DATA, not Java (Article XXVI).

interactions.description: Request information. The most common interaction. Subdivides per-NPC into topics (Article XXXVII.2).
interactions.example: Ask Wang Lin about restrictions.

interactions.description: Transmit knowledge. Source must be willing (Art XXXVIII). May be partial (first layer only).
interactions.example: Wang Lin teaches the player a restriction fragment.

interactions.description: Exchange items/resources. Both parties must agree on value.
interactions.example: Trade spirit stones for herbs with Li Muwan.

interactions.description: Coerce through implied or stated force. Damages relationship, may create grievance, may succeed or provoke.
interactions.example: Threaten a bandit to leave the village.

interactions.description: Watch silently. Updates the observer's beliefs and relationship graph. No interaction initiated. The default for low-relevance targets.
interactions.example: Observe Wang Lin studying restrictions.

interactions.description: Issue a test â€” spar, riddle, trial of courage. May be accepted, refused, or escalated.
interactions.example: Challenge a rival disciple to a spar.

interactions.description: Give something with no explicit expectation of return. May create informal debt (DebtEngine). The most Er Gen of social acts.
interactions.example: The village elder gifts the strange jade.

interactions.description: Request temporary use of an item. Must be returned. Failure to return creates grievance.
interactions.example: Borrow a jade slip from Wang Lin.

interactions.description: Request a copy of a capability (technique/artifact). Governed by Capability Compatibility (Art XXXVIII) â€” willingness, withstand, integrate.
interactions.example: Request a copy of the Ji Realm.

interactions.description: Practice cultivation in proximity. May accelerate both parties. Requires mutual trust.
interactions.example: Cultivate alongside Wang Lin at a spirit vein.

interactions.description: Investigate a location jointly. Shares risk and reward.
interactions.example: Explore the restriction cave with Wang Lin.

interactions.description: Walk/fly to a destination jointly. Enables walk-and-talk (Article XXXII.6).
interactions.example: Wang Lin says 'Come.' and walks toward a mountain.

interactions.description: Combat practice. Usually non-lethal. Wang Lin pulls his strikes; the player will lose; the point is observation.
interactions.example: Spar with Wang Lin (you will lose).

interactions.description: Accompany and protect on a journey. The initiator commits to defense.
interactions.example: Escort a merchant caravan through bandit territory.

interactions.description: Commit to defending the target from a specific threat. Stronger than escort â€” implies an active threat.
interactions.example: Protect Wang Lin's family while he is absent.

interactions.description: Pay for services. Transactional but may build relationship over time.
interactions.example: Hire a guide for the journey to Heng Yue.

interactions.description: Invite to join a faction/sect/group. May be accepted, refused, or turned back on the initiator.
interactions.example: The Heng Yue recruiter recruits village youth.

interactions.description: Look into a mystery, person, or location. Yields information.
interactions.example: Investigate the strange jade's origin.

interactions.description: Reveal something personal â€” a feeling, a secret, a past act. High-trust interaction. May deepen or fracture relationship.
interactions.example: Confess to Li Muwan that you stole the jade.

interactions.description: State falsehood. May be believed, doubted, or caught. If caught, grievance + trust loss. NPCs lie too (Xu Liguo).
interactions.example: Lie to the village elder about where you found the herb.

interactions.description: Mislead through implication or omission. Subtler than lying. Harder to catch.
interactions.example: Deceive Xu Liguo about your cultivation level.

interactions.description: Bargain toward an agreement. Iterative â€” proposals and counters. The core of Article XXXVI.3.
interactions.example: Negotiate with a sect elder for access to the library.

interactions.description: Invoke an outstanding favor (Art XXXIV.2). The debtor is socially obligated to reciprocate.
interactions.example: Wang Lin calls the life-saving favor to justify teaching the Ji Realm.

interactions.description: Honor a called favor, or proactively repay an outstanding debt.
interactions.example: The player repays Wang Lin by protecting his family.

interactions.description: Alert the target to a danger. One word, no explanation (Wang Lin: 'Don't.'). May be heeded or ignored.
interactions.example: Wang Lin warns the player about a canonical danger.

interactions.description: Share hidden knowledge. High-trust. Changes the target's beliefs significantly.
interactions.example: Wang Lin reveals the Heaven Defying Bead's nature.

interactions.description: Transmit a memory directly (via jade slip or soul technique). The target experiences it as if present.
interactions.example: Wang Lin shares a memory of his family.

interactions.description: Collaborative learning. Different from cultivate_together â€” focused on a specific subject (restrictions, alchemy).
interactions.example: Study restriction patterns with Wang Lin.

interactions.description: Silent shared meditation. Builds spiritual rapport. No words exchanged.
interactions.example: Meditate with Qing Shui (comfortable silence).

interactions.description: Ask for something â€” an item, a service, a favor. The general form; specific requests (copy, borrow, teach) are specializations.
interactions.example: Request advice from Wang Lin.

interactions.description: Propose something â€” an item, labor, information. The mirror of request.
interactions.example: Offer to help repair the irrigation ditch.

interactions.description: Show a technique or skill without teaching it. The target may learn through observation (Knowledge System).
interactions.example: Wang Lin demonstrates the Ji Realm once: 'Watch.'

interactions.description: Move with the target without commitment. May be welcomed, tolerated, or rejected.
interactions.example: Follow Wang Lin at a distance.

interactions.description: Physically obstruct. May escalate to combat.
interactions.example: Block a bandit's path.

interactions.description: Move toward the target to initiate interaction. Stage 4-5 of the consent pipeline (Article XXXII consent).
interactions.example: Wang Lin approaches the player.

interactions.description: End the interaction and depart. Always available. The NPC resumes their schedule (Art XXXII.1a courtesy expires).
interactions.example: The player selects 'Leave' â€” Wang Lin resumes cultivating.

interactions.description: Beast-specific. Stalk and kill for food or resource. Beasts use this; cultivators may use it on beasts.
interactions.example: A spirit wolf hunts a spirit rabbit.

interactions.description: Item-specific. Eat/drink/absorb. The item is destroyed; the consumer gains its effect.
interactions.example: Consume a Qi Gathering Pill.

interactions.description: Plant-specific. Gather the plant's yield. May destroy the plant or (with skill) leave it to regrow.
interactions.example: Harvest a Spirit Herb.

interactions.description: Transform a material into a product (alchemy, forging). Requires a workstation.
interactions.example: Refine spirit herbs into a pill at the alchemy furnace.

interactions.description: Study an item/location closely. Perception-tier-gated. May reveal hidden properties.
interactions.example: Examine the strange jade (reveals spiritual residue at Foundation Establishment perception).

_extensibility: This catalog is not closed. New interactions may be added as the project encounters canon situations that don't fit existing verbs â€” but each addition must be justified (why does this not fit an existing verb?) and must apply universally (not special-cased to one entity). Article XVI: build systems, not features.
_unification_note: This catalog unifies: (1) the old affordance_schema (Art XXVIII â€” what an entity CAN do), (2) the social_engines_schema (Art XXXI.1 â€” what an NPC WANTS to do), and (3) the conversation_affordances_per_npc (Art XXXII.2 â€” what the player sees). All three are now views into this single catalog. An entity's data specifies which interactions it exposes; the NPC's reasoning determines which it initiates; the player's UI shows which are currently possible.
